title: The Border Campaign of 1950
summary: An important victory that helped liberate the northern border region, opening communication lines with friendly countries and the world.

slides.text: In 1950, to break the blockade by French troops, Uncle Ho and General Vo Nguyen Giap decided to open a campaign in the northern border region.
slides.imagePrompt: Uncle Ho and General Vo Nguyen Giap looking at a map on a wooden table in a cave, candles, strategic atmosphere, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Uncle Ho directly went to the front lines to encourage the soldiers. The image of Uncle Ho sitting on a mountain observing the battle has become a symbol that lives on forever.
slides.imagePrompt: Uncle Ho in a simple brown suit, sitting on a rock with binoculars, looking at the distant mountains, sunrise, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Our troops bravely attacked and captured positions such as Dong Khe and That Khe. French troops had to flee in chaos, leaving behind many weapons.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese soldiers charging through smoke, French soldiers retreating, rugged mountain terrain, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: This victory helped our country no longer be isolated, opening its arms to receive support from international friends for the resistance.
slides.imagePrompt: A large iron gate opening on a mountain road, trucks carrying supplies entering, people waving, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Who directly went to the front lines to encourage soldiers in the Border Campaign of 1950?
What was the greatest significance of the Border Victory of 1950?
options: Liberating the border region, opening international routes
slides.answer: Liberating the border region, opening international routes
